qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unbounded memory allocation in default memory storage allows remote denial-of-service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies default memoryStorage() buffering successful oversized uploads via concat-stream with no fileSize limit. The advisory-described root cause, confirmed by the referenced patch, is missing request error/aborted/close handling in lib/make-middleware.js when a client drops a multipart upload connection.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory root cause. GHSA-v52c-386h-88mc concerns Multer resource exhaustion when a client drops a file upload connection, fixed by adding request error/aborted/close handling and destroying busboy in lib/make-middleware.js. The only qualifying finding concerns a separate unbounded memory buffering path for completed large uploads through default memoryStorage().
evidence: Advisory summary: Multer versions < 2.1.0 allow DoS by dropping connection during file upload.
Referenced patch cccf0fe0e64150c4f42ccf6654165c0d66b9adab adds req error/aborted/close handlers and busboy.destroy(err) in lib/make-middleware.js.
Finding report cwe-770-unbounded-memory-buffering describes MemoryStorage._handleFile piping file.stream into concat-stream without maxSize and busboy default fileSize=Infinity.
The finding's reproduction uploads a complete 100MB multipart file and verifies req.file.buffer length; it does not exercise dropped-connection handling.
